creator: jdc@koitsu.org
text:
(In reply to Chris Hutchinson from comment #1)
This is faulty advice.  The sysctl is only available if one's kernel config includes "option TCP_RFC7413", which isn't in GENERIC by default because the feature is considered experimental (i.e. most FreeBSD boxes will not have this option, thus not have the sysctl).  A quick review of stable/11 sys/conf and amd64/conf also says this is the case there as well, ditto with a FreeBSD forum post about this problem.  References for my statement:

* https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base?view=revision&revision=292823
* https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/stable/10/sys/netinet/tcp_fastopen.c?revision=292823&view=markup#l27 (read all the way to line 99)
* https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/stable/10/sys/conf/files?revision=323223&view=markup#l3580
* https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/stable/10/sys/conf/options?revision=322744&view=markup#l450
* https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/59367/
* https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/59348/  (faulty advice given at end of thread, re: add to /boot/loader.conf)

Also, I just found Bug 217288 which has been open for the past 9 months, so it looks like the one I opened here was a duplicate.  I did not find that bug because it lacked the word "fastopen" in the summary.  I will mark this as a duplicate.
